name: Swedish Cuisine
description: The flavors of Sweden — from meatballs and cardamom buns to aquavit and herring.

questions.q: Which ubiquitous Swedish dish, often served with lingonberry jam, is a staple of the IKEA cafeteria menu?

source.notes: Köttbullar — IKEA sells hundreds of millions annually.

questions.q: A traditional Swedish smörgåsbord is what kind of meal?
choices: A buffet of many small dishes
A hot-pot where everyone cooks at the table

source.notes: Literally 'table of open sandwiches.'

questions.q: Which spice flavors the Swedish cream bun known as a semla?

source.notes: Cardamom is a signature Swedish baking spice.

questions.q: Which popular Swedish vodka brand, now sold worldwide, began marketing flavored vodkas in the 1980s?

source.notes: Produced in Åhus, Sweden.

questions.q: Pickled herring is a centerpiece of which major Swedish feast?

source.notes: Sill is iconic at Midsummer (Midsommar).

questions.q: The Daim candy bar, which pairs chocolate with a crunchy caramel interior, comes from Sweden. Which nut is blended into the caramel?

source.notes: Made by Marabou (now Mondelēz).

questions.q: Lingonberries are closest in flavor to what North American berry?

source.notes: Tart, red, very similar to cranberry — often served with meatballs.

questions.q: Hasselback potatoes, roasted with thin accordion-like slices, were invented at a Stockholm restaurant in which century?

source.notes: Created at Restaurang Hasselbacken in the 1950s.

questions.q: Gravlax is a Scandinavian classic: raw salmon cured with salt, sugar, and which herb?

source.notes: Often served with hovmästarsås, a mustard-dill sauce.

questions.q: The infamous fermented-herring delicacy 'surströmming' is typically eaten in what way?
choices: Wrapped in flatbread with onion and potato

source.notes: Traditionally eaten in tunnbröd with onion and boiled potato.

questions.q: Which cookie, known as a 'raspberry cave,' is a classic Swedish treat?

source.notes: Hallongrotta literally translates as 'raspberry cave.'

questions.q: Sweden's officially celebrated Cinnamon Bun Day falls on which date?

source.notes: Kanelbullens dag, established 1999.

questions.q: Swedes celebrate Waffle Day every year on which date?

source.notes: Våffeldagen — coincides with the Feast of the Annunciation.

questions.q: Which dark-yellow spice gives the Gotland specialty saffranspannkaka its distinctive color?

source.notes: Saffron pancake from the island of Gotland.

questions.q: The classic Swedish dish 'toast Skagen' is a mix of shrimp, mayonnaise, and dill on toasted bread. In which region did it originate?

source.notes: Named after the Danish port of Skagen; invented by restaurateur Tore Wretman.

questions.q: On St. Lucia's Day (December 13), Swedes bake saffron buns shaped like what?

source.notes: Lussekatter — shaped like a rolled 'S' with raisins in each curl.

questions.q: Which caraway-flavored distilled spirit is common at Swedish holiday tables?

source.notes: Aquavit, often flavored with caraway or dill.

questions.q: Crispbread, or 'knäckebröd,' is made from which grain?

source.notes: Traditional Swedish rye crispbread.

questions.q: What is the most prized wild mushroom in Swedish cooking, often foraged at the end of summer?

source.notes: Known in Swedish as kantarell.

questions.q: The Swedish casserole 'Flying Jacob' (Flygande Jakob) includes chicken, bacon, banana, cream, chili sauce, and which nut?

source.notes: Created in 1976 by air-freight worker Ove Jacobsson — hence the name.

questions.q: A staple 'pea soup on Thursday' tradition in Sweden is paired with which dessert?

source.notes: Ärtsoppa och pannkakor — a long-standing Thursday meal tradition.

questions.q: Västerbotten cheese, a hard cow's-milk cheese, is aged for roughly how long before sale?

source.notes: Produced only in Burträsk, Västerbotten.
